description: John Zenon, a 35-year-old agricultural groundskeeper, was exposed to an organophosphate insecticide and presents with cholinergic toxidrome (SLUDGE syndrome) and profound bradycardia. As the occupational health physician, complete the emergency clinical documentation in GNU Health: record the specific organophosphate toxic effect diagnosis, perform a clinical evaluation documenting the bradycardia, prescribe the Atropine antidote, order at least two toxicity monitoring labs, and schedule a follow-up appointment within 1 to 5 days. Login: admin / gnusolidario.
